titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Douglas Crase and Frank Polach Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 22, 2019 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Douglas Crase and Frank Polach papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, correspondence, photographs, research materials, artwork, and other papers by, to, or relating to Douglas Crase and Frank Polach. Also includes materials materials relating to Rupert Barnaby and Dwight Ripley.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Martin Greenblatt Foundation Book Fund in American Literature, 2014.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 83, 86 (film): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 85 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 87 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Douglas Crase and Frank Polach Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Douglas Crase and Frank Polach Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Douglas Crase and Frank Polach
bioghist.p: Douglas Crase is an American poet and writer. His husband, Frank Polach, is a poet, gardener, and former University Librarian at Rutgers, the State University of New Jersey.

bioghist.head: Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby
bioghist.p: Dwight Ripley was an English artist and patron of the arts associated with the New York School and the Tibor de Nagy Gallery. His life partner was Rupert Barneby, an English botanist.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

did.unittitle: De Evia, Edgar - Ed White's Friend
De Leo, Hank - pen-and-ink for Doug and Frank
Democratic State Central Committee of Michigan
Diget, Patricia Denny - cousin David's 2d wife
Fulton, Alice and Hank de Leo
Handy, Cortlandt W. - Parker Handy Scholarship Donor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "A Garland for John Hollander: October 28, 1989"--assembled by Natalie Charkow and J.D. McClatchy--along with a poem by Douglas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "For James Merill: A Birthday Tribute," assembled by McClatchy for Merill's sixtieth birthday in 1986, which includes a poem by Douglas. A thank you note inscribed from Merrill in "Eight Bits" (folded sheet in wrappers) is laid in.

did.unittitle: Political Reform Commission of the Michigan Democratic Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Writings, card files, and related documentation
Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items. Including Crase college notebooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items. Including Crase college notebooks.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material from The Art and Romance of Dwight Ripley, Ruperti Imagines and Both.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material, drafts, and galleys from Both, as well as assorted magazines and publications.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts and a record of submissions (index card box).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten journals and notebooks, along with notes and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings published in magazine, catalogs, anthologies, and books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Douglas Crase published in magazines, catalogs, anthologies, books, and other outlets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Marjorie Welish, John Ash, Trevor Winkfield, Deborah Rosenthal, Jed Perl, Arnold Kaufman, Douglas Crase Mark Bibbins, Robert Dash, and Margaret Fuller among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by John Koethe, Kenneth Koch, Thomas Spence Smith, Martin Earl, George Bradley, Tony Smith, and Ramon Gutierrez, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Nathan Kernan, John Koethe, David Lehman, Steven Meyer, Mary Kinzie, Susan Mitchell, Lawrence Joseph, Rachel Hadas, and Peter Haaren among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts, reviews, and other writings by Douglas Crase, John Ashbery, James Schulyer, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes genealogical and historical notes and research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes diplomas, award certificates, broadsides, and other personal papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a painting by Robert Dash, photographs, the May 4, 1976 issue of the The New York Times announcing Pulitzer prizes for the year (autographed by John Ashbery), along with other oversize materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This artwork depicts the view from James Schuyler's window at Chelsea Hotel--it is framed and inscribed to Douglas Crase. The drawing was used for the cover of James Schuyler and Darragh Park's Two Journals (Tibor de Nagy Editions, 1995).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence between Frank Pollach and people including Georges Abawi, Bobbi Angell, and John Ashbery, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence between Frank Pollach and many people including Susan Fraser, John Hawkins, James Hunter, Nathan Kernan, Nancy Litwin, Gerard Malanga, Budd Myers, Charles North, Charles, and John Yau, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes house, yard, and garden photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, menus, clippings, ephemera, and other documents, along with extensive notes and research about gardening and plants.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes poems and other writings, along with notes, clippings, and other documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of family and friends of Douglas Crase and Frank Pollach.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35 mm color slides (4 slide trays - "Deep Space", "Sagg '76, '77, '78", "Sagg 80–81", [untitled].

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains A Family Landscape (1980) which is a 16 mm film with sound. Includes Chesebrough-Pond centennial promo filmed at Madoo in 1979 and scenes of Robert Dash painting. Filmscript is by Douglas Crase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs taken at Robert Dash's home in Sagaponack, Long Island; photographic negatives and contact sheets, snapshots from Frank and Douglas' Chelsea and Carley Brook homes and elsewhere; magazines and clippings about Robert Dash.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ripley, Dwight. Ripley, Dwight—NYBG archives (New York Botanical Garden). Ripley, Dwight—will. Ripley, Dwight—estate. Ripley, Dwight—misc. information; Ripley, Alice Louise; Ripley, Dwight—named plants; Ripley, Harry Dwight Dillon; Ripley art collection; Ripley—drawings & collectors; DR to Draguto (i.e., Rupert Barneby)—ca.1932. Handwritten 2 pp. letter.; DR letters to Rupert Barneby—1939–1941 transcribed; DR letters to RB—1939–1941; Dwight's will—1950; Ripley ltrs to J.T. Howell—California Acad. Science; DR—Iberian itinerary—1962; DR—etymology notes—ca. 1965–1970; DR letters to Geraldine Barneby; DR to Geraldine Barneby—12/05/1970; Ripley—list of tricks; Ripley—Memento Mori—SemiColon 1:1, p.3.; DR—misc. botanical notes; Ripley—miscellaneous lists; Ripley—financial statements; Ripley Realty Co; DR—Plaza Stationary; DR—stories; DR—poems in Polish; Ripley—Spring catalogue; Sunlit Scene—Part I; Sunlit Scene—Part II; Beach at Amagansett; Ripley to Willard Maas and—or Marie Menken (photocopies); Ripley to Marie Menken (photocopies); "Autobiography of Dwight Ripley" (photocopies); Ripley "going to the Ball" (photocopies); Woodlawn cem.—Ripley plot; Loria, Jeffrey—receipt of Pollacks sold by Rupert to pay estate taxes; Maas, Willard—poems, letter to RB, 1943–1944; Manrique, Cesar to DR & RB—–ca. 1966; Ripley, Dillon to Rupert Barneby—12/15/1975; Sandwith, Noel to RB 1965; Ruthven, Todd—1957; Photos of The Creeks—sent by Ossorio to Rupert; Maas, Willard to Ripley & Barneby; Menken, Marie to Ripley;

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tibor de Nagy Gallery Records - NY Archives: DR6. Washington Archives: DR7. re: James I. Merrill; Ripley Collection—Cal.Acad. of Sci. - Ripley Collect.: Cal. Academy of Sci.—DR25; Pocket Diaries—Iberian itinerary - Douglas Crase transcriptions; Ripley Letters—Ransom Center; DR—Art notices; Ripley Am. Rock Garden Soc .Bulletin; DR—Etymological dictionary; A list of plants at the Spinney; DR—Observations on Polish; DR Plaza stationary—notes & poems; Catania; Ripley Bkgrd; Background to DR—drawings; Cazorla Violet; Misc. plants in Both; Colored pencil; Color; Collectors—Dwight drawings; Drawing; Ripley family; Maps, etc.—(e.g. Los Angeles); Research leads; Ripley obits; Ripley—poems; Ripley—miscellaneous poems—copies; Record of species; Ripley—Impressions of Nevada; Ripley—Bibliogr. of publications; Ripley AGS bulletin; Species collected—Rupert & Dwight; Miro' Regalito; Marcel Le Piniec award; Union Pacific; Ripley Raisonné; Ripley—Species epithets; The Spinney; Tibor de Nagy—editions; Vasculum; Translation; CPI conversion table; DR funeral home; Greenhouse; Ripley, Harry grave—William Reid Dick - Ripley; Ripley, Harry—will; Blushing; Harrow; Surrogate's Court—letters testamentary—1974–1979; Surrogate's Court—final letters test'ry—1974–1979; Queer persecution in the 1950s; DR—Gleaves, Crawford—financial account; Unsorted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 five year diaries and 10 "week-at-a-glance" calendars.

did.unittitle: "Cabo de Gata,"1962, ink and colored pencil on paper in frame

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dwight Ripley. "Cabo de Gata,"1962. Ink and colored pencil on paper in frame.

did.unittitle: "Setúbal,"1962, ink and colored pencil on paper in frame

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dwight Ripley. "Setúbal,"1962. Ink and colored pencil on paper in frame.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dwight Ripley. "Ponty and Pinty," 1950. Typewritten poem with ink and colored pencil drawing with collaged element in frame. The drawing is on the back of an order form from a New York City tea store on East 55th Street.; Dwight Ripley. "Boom, No.1," 1951. Ink and gouache on stationery in frame.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rupert Barneby. "Astragalus striatiflorus," ca. 1943–44. Black and white photograph in frame.; "Astragalus striatiflorus M. E. Jones on dune beneath sandstone cliffs, near Kenab, Utah" is typewritten caption pasted on framed photograph. Rupert Barneby. "Cymopterus megacephalus," ca. 1941–45. Black and white photograph in frame.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rupert Barneby. "Gilia ripleyi" April 194o, graphite drawing in frame. According to personal correspondence with Douglas, this was "drawn in a motel near Death Valley in April 1940. It was the first new species Rupert and Dwight ever located, although Rupert was not able to publish its scientific description until 1942. By this time he had already published their finding of Cymopterus ripleyi, which thus became their first published new species. Both publications were in Leaflets of Western Botany. The Gilia had a special place in Rupert's heart. It has since been removed from the genus Gilia and renamed Aliciella ripleyi."; Rupert Barneby. "Self-Portrait," 1944. Collage in frame.; Rupert Barneby. "Portrait of Dwight Ripley," 1944. Collage in frame. "Self-Portrait" and "Portrait of Dwight Ripley" can be viewed as "family portraits." They are created in the style of French poet, critic and collagist Georges Hugnet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Cymopterus megacephalus M. E. Jones on talus under cliffs near Navajo Bridge, Coconino Co., Arizona" is typewritten caption pasted on framed photograph. Rupert Barneby. "Arctomecon humilis," ca. 1941. 3 black and white framed photographs. Also known as the Dwarf Bearclaw Poppy. Rupert and Dwight collected the plant in 1941 and 1942.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rupert Barneby. Dwight Ripley at Frenchman Flat, May 13, 1941. Taken on the day that Cymopterus ripleyi var. saniculoides was found. Framed copy of black and white photograph.

did.unittitle: Douglas Crase research materials on Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No Subnote Content

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose color photographs taken by Dwight Ripley of Mexico.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose color photographs taken by Dwight Ripley of Mexico.

did.unittitle: Photographs taken by Dwight and Rupert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Albums

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 albums of photographs taken by Dwight and Rupert, ca. 1930s and 1940s.

did.unittitle: Books by others and assorted papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 photographs of Louisa Geraldine (Ingham) Barneby Rupert's mother sometimes called "Poppy."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dwight Ripley. "Record of Species," 1941–49. Bound ledger book with handwritten record of species found by Dwight and Rupert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rupert Barneby's Rolodex rotary card file.

did.unittitle: Citations, plaques and awards and other material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Citations, plaques and awards presented to Rupert Barneby; Paired photographs of Douglas Crase and Frank Polach along with photograph of Alfonso Ossorio sculpture (Rupert Barneby designed the arboretum at The Creeks, East Hampton, N.Y. where the sculptures were displayed. The photographs of Douglas and Frank were always displayed in Rupert's bedroom in his Botanical Garden loft. Polish language newspapers in which Dwight Ripley's poems were published during World War II; Other assorted items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Citations, plaques and awards presented to Rupert Barneby.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tufa - Douglas writes of this piece of tufa in Both: "In Rupert's loft there were many curious objects. One that holds my attention in retrospect consisted of a tablet-sized platform, as for a bonsai arrangement, on which sat a porous gray rock that might have been the scale model of a malevolent asteroid. It was a chunk of tufa, the calcareous (as in limestone) deposit from springs and streams that is favored in the rock garden or in the alpine house by plants like Lepidium nanum, is coveted therefore by the rock gardener, and from which Rupert and Dwight constructed their rock-and-tufa cliff at the Spinney."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The larger vasculum was used in 1941 and later when they discovered Cymopterus ripleyi and other plants in Nevada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The smaller and ornate vasculum was used by Dwight and Rupert on expeditions to Spain and North Africa prior to 1939. It held many samples, including Limonium asparagoides that brought back from Algeria.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dylan Thomas Fund. Letter to Dwight Ripley. Dec. 8. 1953. In frame. Letter on Intercultural Publications Inc. stationery thanking Dwight for his "magnificent contribution to The Dylan Thomas Fund. Signed by Tennessee Williams, Thornton Wilder, Arthur Miller, Marianne Moore, W. H. Auden and Wallace Stevens.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barbara Ertter. Rupert Barneby with colleagues, Independence Mountains, Nevada, June 24, 1981. This is a framed copy print. According to Douglas this "is the only photo so far located of Rupert collecting with colleagues after Dwight's death."; Joan Leigh-Fermor. "House party in Sussex, 1935." Framed copy photograph from Clive Fisher, Cyril Connolly: The Life and Times of England's Most Controversial Literary Critic, (1995).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Button. "Nude portrait of Joe LeSeur" in frame, ca. early 1950s? Dylan Thomas Fund. Letter to Dwight Ripley. Dec. 8. 1953. In frame.; Unidentified photographer. "Dwight Ripley," photographic portrait taken at the Plaza Hotel, NYC, ca. 1944.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tibor de Nagy Gallery ledger page, November–December 1950. Color facsimile in frame.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dwight Ripley. "Master Rupert Barneby at Harrow," 1925. Pencil sketch in frame. Peter Markham Scott. "Rupert Barneby," pencil sketch, 1937.
